prompt: A hyper-realistic physical scene inside a dimly lit tailor workshop. A mannequin stands at the center wearing an extremely over-tailored suit — stitched obsessively tight, with hundreds of tiny adjustments, pins, and seams perfectly conforming to every contour of the mannequin’s body. The suit looks unnaturally precise, almost fused to the mannequin, with excessive stitching, layered patches, and intricate corrections visible up close. In the background, slightly out of focus, real human figures stand nearby unable to wear similar suits — fabric hangs awkwardly on them or doesn’t fit at all, suggesting failure to generalize. The workshop is filled with fabric scraps, measuring tapes, and tailoring tools scattered around, reinforcing the obsessive refinement process. Lighting is cinematic and grounded — warm tungsten light focused on the mannequin, darker shadows around the room. The scene feels physically real, like a photograph taken in a real workshop. No text, no symbols, no diagrams, no abstract elements — everything must exist naturally in the physical world. Ultra-realistic, highly detailed textures, shallow depth of field, cinematic composition, magazine-quality photography, grounded and believable.
metaphor: A suit tailored perfectly to a mannequin but unusable for real people, showing perfect fit to training data but poor generalization.
